moduleDescription:
This module implements efficient computations of hash values for diverse Nim types. All the procs are based on these two building blocks:
  • !& proc used to start or mix a hash value, and
  • !$ proc used to finish the hash value.

If you want to implement hash procs for your custom types, you will end up writing the following kind of skeleton of code:

If your custom types contain fields for which there already is a hash proc, you can simply hash together the hash values of the individual fields:
Important: Use -d:nimPreviewHashRef to enable hashing refs. It is expected that this behavior becomes the new default in upcoming versions.
Note: If the type has a == operator, the following must hold: If two values compare equal, their hashes must also be equal.

See also

  • md5 module for the MD5 checksum algorithm
  • base64 module for a Base64 encoder and decoder
  • sha1 module for the SHA-1 checksum algorithm
  • tables module for hash tables

entries.name: Hash
entries.code: Hash = int
entries.description: A hash value. Hash tables using these values should always have a size of a power of two so they can use the and operator instead of mod for truncation of the hash value.

entries.name: `!&`
entries.code: proc `!&`(h: Hash; val: int): Hash {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Mixes a hash value h with val to produce a new hash value.

This is only needed if you need to implement a hash proc for a new datatype.

entries.code: proc `!$`(h: Hash): Hash {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Finishes the computation of the hash value.

This is only needed if you need to implement a hash proc for a new datatype.

entries.name: hashWangYi1
entries.code: proc hashWangYi1(x: int64 | uint64 | Hash): Hash {.inline.}
entries.description:
Wang Yi's hash_v1 for 64-bit ints (see https://github.com/rurban/smhasher for more details). This passed all scrambling tests in Spring 2019 and is simple.

Note: It's ok to define proc(x: int16): Hash = hashWangYi1(Hash(x)).

entries.name: hashData
entries.code:
proc hashData(data: pointer; size: int): Hash {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Hashes an array of bytes of size size.

entries.name: hashIdentity
entries.code: proc hashIdentity[T: Ordinal | enum](x: T): Hash {.inline.}
entries.description: The identity hash, i.e. hashIdentity(x) = x.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[T: Ordinal | enum](x: T): Hash {.inline.}
entries.description: Efficient hashing of integers.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash(x: pointer): Hash {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Efficient hash overload.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[T](x: ptr [T]): Hash {.inline.}
entries.description: Efficient hash overload.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[T](x: ref [T]): Hash {.inline.}
entries.description:
Efficient hash overload.
Important: Use -d:nimPreviewHashRef to enable hashing refs. It is expected that this behavior becomes the new default in upcoming versions.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash(x: float): Hash {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Efficient hashing of floats.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[A](x: openArray[A]): Hash

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[A](x: set[A]): Hash

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash(x: string): Hash {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Efficient hashing of strings.

See also:

  • hashIgnoreStyle
  • hashIgnoreCase

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash(x: cstring): Hash {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Efficient hashing of null-terminated strings.

entries.name: hash
entries.code:
proc hash(sBuf: string; sPos, ePos: int): Hash {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Efficient hashing of a string buffer, from starting position sPos to ending position ePos (included).

hash(myStr, 0, myStr.high) is equivalent to hash(myStr).

entries.name: hashIgnoreStyle
entries.code: proc hashIgnoreStyle(x: string): Hash {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Efficient hashing of strings; style is ignored.

Note: This uses a different hashing algorithm than hash(string).

See also:

  • hashIgnoreCase

    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Efficient hashing of a string buffer, from starting position sPos to ending position ePos (included); style is ignored.

Note: This uses a different hashing algorithm than hash(string).

hashIgnoreStyle(myBuf, 0, myBuf.high) is equivalent to hashIgnoreStyle(myBuf).

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[T: tuple | object | proc | iterator {.closure.}](x: T): Hash

genericParams.name: T
genericParams.types: tuple or object or proc or proc

entries.description: Efficient hash overload.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: proc hash[A](aBuf: openArray[A]; sPos, ePos: int): Hash
entries.description:
Efficient hashing of portions of arrays and sequences, from starting position sPos to ending position ePos (included). There must be a hash proc defined for the element type A.

hash(myBuf, 0, myBuf.high) is equivalent to hash(myBuf).
